channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Spirit Airlines collapses amid rising fuel costs from war on Iran
item.description: US budget carrier Spirit Airlines shuts down after talks for a government bailout failed.

item.title: At least six dead in heavy rains in northeast Brazil, thousands displaced
item.description: Studies show extreme rain has become more frequent in country, as Pernambuco and Paraiba states again battered.

item.title: Khartoum drone strike kills five Sudan NGO reports
item.description: The attack, the second in a week, follows months of relative calm in the city after government forces regained control.

item.title: Ukraine eyes Belarus border activities as Russian strikes persist
item.description: The role of Belarus in Russia's war on Ukraine has provoked sanctions from Kyiv, which says it is ready to respond.

item.title: Syria becomes alternative energy corridor for oil as Hormuz effectively blo
item.description: Syria is receiving hundreds of Iraqi oil trucks hauling crude overland as an alternative energy corridor to Europe.

item.title: Israel’s expanding ‘orange line’ tightens deadly no-go zones in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s expanding “orange line” is deepening movement restrictions across Gaza, raising deadly risks for civilians.

item.title: Arsenal ease past Fulham to pull six points clear of Manchester City
item.description: Viktor Gyokeres scores twice in Arsenal's 3-0 win against Fulham to boost hopes of ending 22-year title wait.

item.title: Spain demands Israel release arrested Gaza flotilla crew member
item.description: Saif Abukeshek was taken to Israel after forces raided the Global Sumid Flotilla off the coast of Greece.

item.title: Devastation of Southern Lebanon continues under ‘ceasefire’
item.description: At least 41 people killed as Israel launches 50 airstrikes on southern Lebanon in 24 hours.

item.title: Yemen reports hijacked oil tanker headed for Somalia
item.description: The incident marks at least the fourth vessel hijacking near Somalia in recent weeks.

item.title: Spain’s Sanchez demands Netanyahu free Spaniard seized on aid flotilla
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez is demanding Israel to release the 'abducted' Spaniard taken from the flotilla.

item.title: Prices surge, jobs disappear as war strains Iran’s economy
item.description: New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei says the country must defeat its enemies in an economic and cultural struggle, as we

item.title: Mexico’s Sinaloa state governor resigns amid US drug trafficking charges
item.description: Ruben Rocha Moya again denies allegations he shielded cartel, says taking 'temporary leave' to defend self.

item.title: Israel’s ‘two-tier’ policing and the crime epidemic in Palestinian towns
item.description: Murder rates in Israel's Palestinian areas soar, fueling allegations of state complicity and neglect.

item.title: Arsenal 3-0 Fulham: Premier League – as it happened
item.description: All the updates, with post-match reaction and interviews, following our text commentary stream.

item.title: The United States of Conspiracy
item.description: Another assassination attempt on Donald Trump reveals mistrust in the media and conspiracy theories fill the gap.

item.title: The dark side of Gaza’s new fancy cafes and restaurants
item.description: The new establishments popping up in the devastated Strip reveal a new genocidal reality.

item.title: Mali probes soldiers suspected of involvement in military base attacks
item.description: Five army personnel, including three active-duty soldiers, identified as suspects in last week's coordinated attacks.

item.title: Israeli air strikes on Lebanon kill 41 people in 24-hours
item.description: Death toll from Israeli attacks on Lebanon since March 2 increases to 2,659, 8,183 injured.

item.title: Flotilla activists return to Istanbul after Israel detains them at sea
item.description: Global Sumud Flotilla activists have arrived in Istanbul after Israel intercepted their Gaza-bound aid convoy at sea.

item.title: Rallies under way as workers gather for International Labour Day
item.description: Workers, activists and trade unions around the world marked May Day with rallies on Friday.

item.title: NATO assessing details of US troop withdrawal from Germany
item.description: US says it plans to pull about 5,000 troops out of Germany over next six to 12 months amid feud with European allies.

item.title: Trump says US Navy acting ‘like pirates’ to enforce Iran blockade
item.description: The US president says the ongoing naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is 'a very profitable business'.

item.title: Ex-Formula One driver turned Paralympic champion Alex Zanardi dies aged 59
item.description: Zanardi, who lost his legs in a racing crash, was credited with helping transform the perception of disability in Italy.

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi pledges deeper energy cooperation with Vietnam
item.description: Takaichi signs six agreements with Vietnam, including on technology, agriculture and space, during a trip to Hanoi.
